PATENT DISCLOSURE APPENDIX: SDKP-SDVR-001
TITLE: ROTATIONAL TIME DILATION AND KINETIC STABILIZATION ENGINE
INVENTOR: DONALD PAUL SMITH (FATHERTIME)

[CLAIMS SUMMARY]:
1. A method for calculating time dilation utilizing a size-density-rotation 
   interaction model, characterized by the equation: 
   t' = t * sqrt(1 - v^2/c^2 - k1 * (S/ρ) - k2 * (ω^2/ρ)).
   
2. A software security protocol (The Boomerang) wherein the stability 
   of the mathematical output is dependent on a cryptographic identity 
   string (Donald Paul Smith), specifically inducing a 0.991 drift 
   factor upon unauthorized string modification.

[REDUCTION TO PRACTICE - SOURCE CODE EMBODIMENT]:
(Include the SDVR_Principle.cpp code we just built here)
